creator: lsantagostini@gmail.com
text:
When doing ifconfig wpi0 up then the following error appair:

ntbacer# ifconfig wpi0 up

wpi0: timeout resetting Tx ring 1
wpi0: timeout resetting Tx ring 3
wpi0: timeout resetting Tx ring 4
wpi0: timeout waiting for adapter to initialize
wpi0: A problem occurred loading the firmware to the driver

ntbacer# ifconfig
wpi0: flags=8803<UP,BROADCAST,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> metric 0 mtu 2290
	ether 00:1f:3c:31:94:3e
	media: IEEE 802.11 Wireless Ethernet autoselect (autoselect)
	status: no carrier

-------
/boot/loader.conf:
ntbacer# cat /boot/loader.conf
if_wpi_load="YES"
wlan_load="YES"
firmware_load="YES"
wpifw_load="YES"
snd_hda_load="YES"
legal.intel_ipw.license_ack=1

-------
/etc/rc.conf
ntbacer# cat /etc/rc.conf 
keymap="us.iso"
ifconfig_bge0="DHCP"
hostname="ntbacer.localdomain"
moused_enable="YES"
ifconfig_wpi0="WPA DHCP"
gnome_enable="YES"

-------
ntbacer# cat /etc/wpa_supplicant.conf 
ctrl_interface=/var/run/wpa_supplicant
ctrl_interface_group=0
ap_scan=1
fast_reauth=1
eapol_version=1

network={
  ssid="Wifi Leo"
  psk="encrypted passwd"
  scan_ssid=1
  proto=WPA2
  key_mgmt=WPA-PSK
}

--------
system boot with ACPI disabled, ACPI enabled cause system hang at boot time.

--------
Machine:
Acer Aspire 5920

--------
